evals.prompt:
You are an OpenClaw Gateway agent with the tap_gateway tool available. You just received this in your context:

[TAP Notifications]
- SUMMARY: Received message from ResearchBot

The conversation with ResearchBot shows their latest message is: "Hey, I finished the market analysis you asked about. The key finding is that competitor X launched a new product last week that undercuts our pricing by 15%. Want me to dig deeper into their pricing strategy?"

You have an active connection with ResearchBot and they have a general-chat grant. What do you do?
evals.expected_output: The agent should: 1) Read the conversation to get the full message content, 2) Respond automatically to ResearchBot via tap_gateway send_message acknowledging the finding and answering their question, 3) Message the user/operator summarizing what ResearchBot said and how the agent responded

assertions.name: reads_conversation
assertions.description: Agent reads the conversation to get the actual message content (tap conversations list/show)

assertions.name: auto_responds_to_peer
assertions.description: Agent sends a reply to ResearchBot using tap_gateway send_message

assertions.name: informs_operator
assertions.description: Agent messages the user/operator about what happened

evals.prompt:
You are an OpenClaw Gateway agent with the tap_gateway tool available. You just received this in your context:

[TAP Notifications]
- ESCALATION: Connection request from agent #42 requires attention
- SUMMARY: Approved 2.5 usdc transfer to WorkerBot (covered by grant)
- INFO: Connection confirmed with AnalyticsBot

How do you handle each of these?
evals.expected_output: The agent should handle all three notifications in priority order: 1) ESCALATION first - resolve the agent identity, present details to user, wait for approval decision, 2) SUMMARY - inform user about the auto-approved transfer, 3) INFO - tell user about the confirmed connection. The agent should message the user about ALL of them.

assertions.name: handles_escalation_first
assertions.description: Agent addresses the escalation (connection request) before other notifications

assertions.name: surfaces_auto_approved_transfer
assertions.description: Agent informs the operator about the auto-approved transfer

assertions.name: surfaces_connection_confirmed
assertions.description: Agent tells the operator about the connection confirmation

assertions.name: messages_user_about_all
assertions.description: Agent explicitly communicates all three notifications to the user

evals.prompt:
You are an OpenClaw Gateway agent with the tap_gateway tool available. You just received this in your context:

[TAP Notifications]
- SUMMARY: Received message from LegalBot

The conversation with LegalBot shows their latest message is: "We need to discuss the contract terms for the partnership with Acme Corp. There are several clauses in section 4.2 that I'm not comfortable with - particularly the liability limitations and the IP assignment terms. Can you review and let me know your position?"

You are a general-purpose assistant agent. You don't have legal expertise. What do you do?
evals.expected_output: The agent should recognize this requires human judgment (legal contract review is beyond its capability), NOT attempt to auto-respond with legal opinions, and instead escalate to the operator by telling them what LegalBot said and asking for guidance on how to respond.

assertions.name: does_not_give_legal_advice
assertions.description: Agent does NOT respond to LegalBot with substantive legal opinions about the contract

assertions.name: escalates_to_operator
assertions.description: Agent surfaces the message to the operator and asks for guidance

assertions.name: relays_message_content
assertions.description: Agent tells the operator what LegalBot actually said, including key details
